zlomkova_zoo.title: Fraction Zoo
zlomkova_zoo.description: Students help zoo animals by correctly dividing food using fractions.

header.subtitle: Learn how to set up a 3D printer!

ui.world_sub: Energy from this world is in your collection!
ui.win_msg: You collected {count} energy units! Now you know how to set up a 3D printer for any job. 🎉
ui.win_sources: 📚 Facts from: Sinterit.com · All3DP.com · Ultimaker.com · Polymaker Wiki

mascot.idle: Choose your settings and let's print!
mascot.thinking: Hmm, which material will you pick?
mascot.great: The customer is thrilled! Excellent work!
mascot.ok: The customer is happy. What could you try next time?
mascot.curious: Interesting result! What would you change next time?

results.curious: Interesting result – what would you try differently?
results.bad: Hmm, the result doesn't match what the customer wanted. What could you change?

PLA.title: PLA – Hard plastic
PLA.text: 🌽 PLA is made from cornstarch and sugarcane – one of the most eco-friendly plastics!

PETG.title: PETG – Strong plastic
PETG.text: 💪 PETG withstands temperatures up to 80 °C and is stronger than PLA. Great for load-bearing parts.

TPU.title: TPU – Rubbery material
TPU.text: 🤸 TPU can stretch up to 5× its length! Like plastic rubber – flexible and tough.

facts.text: The printer builds layer by layer – like an onion!
facts.detail: Each layer is 0.1–0.3 mm. Thinner layer = smoother surface, but longer print. Half the thickness = double the time!
PLA comes from renewable sources – cornstarch or sugarcane. Gentle on the environment.

facts.text: Infill determines both strength and weight!
facts.detail: 0–1 % = light and hollow. 100 % = full and strong. Keys and tools need lots of infill!
Slowly = nicer result. Quickly = saves time. For decorations we print slowly, for prototypes quickly.
TPU (thermoplastic polyurethane) can stretch up to 5× its length. That's why it's used for toys, phone cases, and guards.
PLA softens around 60 °C – not suitable for items in a car or near a stove. PETG can even handle a hot oven!
Unlike machining where material is removed, a 3D printer only adds what is needed.
For decorative objects, PLA is best – it sands and paints easily. PETG and ABS are rougher.

challenges.name: Rubber duck
challenges.desc: A toy for a small child. It must be soft and flexible!
challenges.hint: 💡 TPU stretches up to 5× its length. It's like real rubber!

mats.good: ✅ TPU – stretches up to 5× and bounces back. Perfect for a toy!
mats.bad: ❌ Hard plastic would be like a rock. The child would be disappointed!

inf.good: ✅ Medium infill = the right weight for the duck.
inf.ok: 💛 This infill works, but medium would be better.

challenges.name: Winner's trophy
challenges.desc: A nice trophy for the shelf. A beautiful surface is important!
challenges.hint: 💡 PLA gives the smoothest surfaces. Hollow infill saves material.

mats.good: ✅ PLA = beautiful smooth surface. Made from corn! 🌽
mats.ok: 💛 PETG works, but PLA would be smoother.
mats.bad: ❌ A rubbery trophy would be bent and weird!

inf.good: ✅ Hollow = light, saves material. The trophy holds nothing!
inf.ok: 💛 This infill works, hollow would be better.

spd.ok: 💛 Quickly works, but slowly = smoother surface.

challenges.name: Dartboard
challenges.desc: A solid dartboard for the wall. It must withstand darts and impacts!

mats.good: ✅ PETG is stronger than PLA. The board will take many hits!
mats.bad: ❌ A rubber dartboard would wave with every dart!

inf.good: ✅ 100 % infill = maximum strength. The board needs it!
inf.ok: 💛 Medium infill works, but solid would be safer.
inf.bad: ❌ A hollow dartboard will break on the first hit!

challenges.name: Finger puppet
challenges.desc: A puppet for the theatre. Must be comfortable on a finger and slightly flexible!
challenges.hint: 💡 For fingers we want flexible material, not hard plastic.

mats.good: ✅ TPU is flexible – comfortable on the finger and won't snap!
mats.ok: 💛 PLA works, but can feel hard on a finger.
mats.bad: ❌ PETG is too rigid – the puppet would be hard to put on.

inf.good: ✅ Hollow = light, loose puppet. The finger moves freely.
inf.bad: ❌ A solid puppet would be heavy and stiff.

challenges.name: Game die
challenges.desc: A die for a board game. It must be precise and solid!
challenges.hint: 💡 Precision = nice print. Strength = high infill.

mats.good: ✅ PETG – solid and the numbers will be precise!
mats.ok: 💛 PLA works, but PETG would be stronger.
mats.bad: ❌ A rubber die? It would deform every time you throw it!

inf.good: ✅ Solid die = won't get crushed even under pressure!
inf.bad: ❌ A hollow die would crack on the first throw!

spd.good: ✅ Slowly = nicer numbers on the die.
spd.ok: 💛 Quickly works, but numbers may be rougher.

challenges.name: Windowsill plant pot
challenges.desc: A pretty pot for the window. Light and decorative!
challenges.hint: 💡 Decoration = PLA + slow print + hollow infill.

mats.good: ✅ PLA = beautiful smooth surface for a decorative pot!
mats.ok: 💛 PETG works, but PLA would look nicer.
mats.bad: ❌ A rubber pot would sag under the weight of soil!

inf.good: ✅ Hollow = light. The windowsill can handle it!
inf.bad: ❌ A solid pot would be heavy as a stone!

spd.ok: 💛 Quickly works, but the surface will be rougher.

challenges.name: Phone stand
challenges.desc: A desk stand. It must hold the phone firmly and be stable!
challenges.hint: 💡 Stability = enough infill. PETG handles pressure better.

mats.good: ✅ PETG is stronger – the stand will hold even a heavy tablet!
mats.bad: ❌ A rubber stand = the phone keeps tipping over!

inf.good: ✅ Medium infill = good stability without wasting material.
inf.ok: 💛 Solid infill is unnecessary, but it works.
inf.bad: ❌ A hollow stand will collapse under the phone!

challenges.name: Key hook
challenges.desc: A wall hook. It must hold keys and be screwed in firmly!
challenges.hint: 💡 Strength is key – keys are heavy!

mats.good: ✅ PETG = solid and tough. Keys won't fall!
mats.bad: ❌ A rubber hook? The keys would stretch it down!

inf.good: ✅ 100 % infill = the hook won't pull from the wall!
inf.bad: ❌ A hollow hook with keys will snap!

challenges.name: Soap dispenser pump
challenges.desc: A replacement pump for liquid soap. Must be waterproof and solid!
challenges.hint: 💡 PETG resists water and chemicals better than PLA.

mats.good: ✅ PETG resists water and soap. Right choice for the bathroom!
mats.ok: 💛 PLA absorbs moisture over time – not ideal.
mats.bad: ❌ A rubber pump wouldn't function properly!

inf.good: ✅ Solid infill = no pores for water to seep into.
inf.bad: ❌ Hollow pump – soap would get inside!

challenges.name: Tea light candle holder
challenges.desc: A pretty holder for the table. A beautiful look is the main goal!
challenges.hint: 💡 PLA + slow print = the most beautiful decorative result.

mats.good: ✅ PLA = smooth beautiful surface for the candle holder!
mats.ok: 💛 PETG works, but the surface won't be as smooth.

inf.bad: ❌ A solid holder is unnecessarily heavy.

spd.good: ✅ Slow print = wonderful candle holder details!
spd.ok: 💛 Quickly works, but details will be rougher.

challenges.name: Pencil holder
challenges.desc: A desk organiser for school supplies. Must be stable and nice looking!
challenges.hint: 💡 Stability = medium infill. PLA for a nice appearance.

mats.good: ✅ PLA = beautiful and smooth. Perfect for a school desk!
mats.bad: ❌ A rubber holder will bend and pencils will fall!

inf.good: ✅ Medium infill = good stability, won't tip over.
inf.bad: ❌ A hollow holder full of pencils will topple!

spd.good: ✅ Slowly = nicer result for the classroom!

challenges.name: Test tube rack
challenges.desc: A rack for the school lab. Must hold test tubes firmly!
challenges.hint: 💡 Lab = strength and resistance. PETG resists chemicals!

mats.good: ✅ PETG resists acids and bases in the lab!
mats.ok: 💛 PLA can be damaged by contact with chemicals.
mats.bad: ❌ A rubber rack bends – test tubes will tip over!

inf.good: ✅ Solid infill = maximum strength for a safe lab!
inf.bad: ❌ A hollow rack will collapse with test tubes in it!

challenges.name: Map puzzle piece
challenges.desc: Part of an educational continents puzzle. Light and good-looking!
challenges.hint: 💡 Puzzle = PLA + hollow infill + slow for details.

mats.good: ✅ PLA = smooth surface, nice colours, eco-friendly!
mats.bad: ❌ A rubber puzzle piece will warp and won't fit!

inf.good: ✅ Hollow = light piece. The puzzle is easy to assemble!
inf.bad: ❌ Solid puzzle pieces are heavy and tire children out!

spd.ok: 💛 Quickly works, but edges will be rougher.

challenges.name: Millimetre ruler
challenges.desc: A precise ruler for technical drawing. It must have accurate mm!

mats.good: ✅ PETG = solid and precise ruler that won't warp!
mats.ok: 💛 PLA works, but may bend over time.
mats.bad: ❌ A rubber ruler? Every measurement will be different!

inf.good: ✅ Solid ruler = rigid, won't flex during drawing!
inf.bad: ❌ A hollow ruler will flex and measurements won't match!

spd.ok: 💛 Quickly works, but markings may be less precise.

challenges.name: Rocket wall decoration
challenges.desc: A classroom decoration. Must be beautiful and light!
challenges.hint: 💡 For beautiful decorations: PLA + slow print + low infill!

mats.good: ✅ PLA = smoothest surfaces for a classroom decoration!
mats.bad: ❌ A rubber rocket would hang crooked!

inf.good: ✅ Hollow = light and economical. The decoration carries nothing!
inf.bad: ❌ A solid decoration = unnecessarily heavy and expensive!

spd.good: ✅ Slowly = most beautiful result. The class will love it!
spd.ok: 💛 Quickly works, but the surface will be rougher.
